column11: I’m just following up on my email from last week. I want to know if now is a better time to talk.
column13:
Hello Patrick,

It was great connecting with you today, after Charlie Walton’s intro. As I shared, I recently relocated to Boston after a career leading complex digital transformation and strategic identity projects in Latin America.

My background is rooted in technical product leadership and driving large-scale digital transformation. Most recently, I successfully led a bank-owned Digital Identity and fraud prevention platform (Soy Yo) as CEO. I drove it to a successful exit by scaling the user base to 3M+ and building a trusted ecosystem through key alliances with Thales, Mastercard, LexisNexis, TransUnion, and Experian. I also bring experience scaling infrastructure as Chief Digital & Technology Officer at Avianca and CIO at Telefónica , and strategic governance as a Board Member for major credit card issuers and a cross-border payments firm.

As an MIT Sloan alum, I am confident my expertise in Identity, Security, and Fintech can significantly contribute to aligning technology with strategic business goals and driving product innovation.

I would greatly appreciate an introduction to any leaders in Product or Technology at Ping Identity. As discussed, I have attached my CV for your reference.

Thank you again for your time and connection.

column11:
Hello Victor. Thanks for connecting. Jorge Arbesú introduced us. 

Would you consider your Recorded Future to be the platform  for your emerging autonomous threat operations?
I ask because it seems quite comprehensive and represents a lot of potential for future innovation.

My name is Santiago Aldana, previous CEO of SoYo, the bank/digital identity and fraud prevention platform where we partnerede with Mastercard / Ekata. After a successful exit, I am evaluating product leadership roles within the financial crimes technology segment. 

Attached is my resume.

When may you have availability to discuss your Chief Strategy Officer role?

Thank you.
column13:
Dear Victor,

As you know, Jorge Arbesú suggested I reach out, believing my executive background in the FinTech fraud and Digital Identity ecosystem is highly relevant to Recorded Future’s strategic goals.

I have been following Recorded Future’s work—including the "2025 State of Threat Intelligence" report—and understand the critical need for intelligence to mitigate risk and guide strategic security investments at the board level.

My experience includes:

Executive Fraud Platform Leadership: As CEO of SoyYo (a fraud prevention platform owned by the top three Colombian banks), I had P&L responsibility for B2B security products, partnering directly with global vendors like LexisNexis (ThreatMetrix) and Mastercard (Ekata/Nudata) to mitigate third-party and APP fraud at scale.

Strategic & Technical Governance: I bring C-level experience in large-scale digital transformation and board-level strategy, which is key to driving the adoption of threat intelligence in highly regulated financial services.

Future of AI: I am currently focused on Agentic AI and its imminent impact on both the generation and real-time prevention of sophisticated attacks, a topic relevant to your product roadmap.

I am keen to explore senior Product Management or General Management opportunities. Would you be open to a brief 20-minute discussion next week to see how my vision aligns with Recorded Future’s growth?

Please excuse me if I’m being intense. I don’t mean to be. This will be my last email on the matter.

Thank you,

Thanks for your meeting last week. You’ve given me a lot to think about. I have explored more on the wonderful things you’ve done at SBUR, and upon further reflection, this is definitely something that interests me a lot. 

As you suggested, let me send you a small description (one pager) on my experience. I guess the following should be short enough to guide you with my background:

I have a background in leading digital transformations and driving innovation in corporations and startups. My experience includes successfully leading a digital identity and fraud prevention startup as CEO, where I grew the company and established key partnerships with Mastercard, LexisNexis, and Experian, among others, driving it to a successful exit through selling it to the main Payment Service Provider. I also served as Chief Digital & Technology Officer at Avianca, CIO at Telefónica, and found and led a mobile telecommunications startup which had a successful exit selling it to the main bank in Colombia, as it planned to offer financial inclusion to underserved communities. 
 
As an MIT Sloan alum, I am confident I can significantly contribute aligning technology with strategic business goals, improving the customer experience, product development and driving operational efficiency. I’ve been a mentor for founders at Endeavor Colombia, and I also served as a board member in the Colombian Fintech Association, helping in the development of the fintech sector in the country. 
 
Thanks again for all your help. How would you go about doing that if you were me? For example, can you recommend someone I should speak to next?
